comment:
Greeting
Hi, Marina! How have you been? I’ve found a couple of pictures, I was thinking we could use them for our project “Best Presents”. I’d like to discuss them with you.

Brief description, justifying the choice of the photos for the project.
Well, I think the first photo can be used for our project because there is a woman receiving a handmade greeting card from her daughter and handmade gifts are really popular among children. Also, creating presents helps develop their creativity.
In contrast, the second photo works because there is a man giving his loved-one a small wrapped gift and flowers and it is a very common practice here in Russia to give women flowers.

Differences, justifying the choice of the photos for the project.
So, together, the pictures illustrate two different kinds of presents: a handmade present and a purchased gift.
In the first photo the little girl has drawn a picture of a heart on a handmade card, whereas in the second photo the man has simply wrapped a gift in a box and tied a bow around it.
Furthermore, in the first photo the gift has a message written on it for the girl’s mother, whereas in the second photo there aren’t any tags or messages or anything on the present.

Advantages and disadvantages (1-2) of the two kinds of presents.
Well, I can see some advantages and disadvantages of the two kinds of presents. In my personal view, an advantage of handmade gifts is that they are free. The disadvantage is that they might be useless and are often thrown away after some time. What’s good about buying presents for someone is that later when you see them using what you bought for them, you’ll be happy too. The downside is that often people expect you to spend a lot of money on them

Your opinion on the subject of the project - whether you would like to give someone a handmade present and why.
To tell the truth, I wouldn’t like to give someone a handmade present. The only reason I don’t want to do it because most people don’t like to get them.

Closing Phrase
Anyway, I’ll send these photos to you and I want to know if you agree with me. That’s all for now. Talk to you later, bye.
